purpose: Craft examples for ruined settlements, shelters, scavenging, resource pressure, convoy danger, and survival-rule stories.
source_priority_rules: Shelters, factions, vehicles, weapons, mutations, devices, and resource limits must come from source or locked payload.
Survival pressure should come from scarcity, exposure, route risk, environmental collapse, or a compromised safe zone.
Do not add apocalypse lore, military background, radiation mechanics, or monsters unless the source supports them.
genre_boundary: Track shelter safety, route safety, resource count, and who controls the current space.
Visible action should show cost: fuel, food, medicine, air, shelter access, or time.
Not every beat is gunfire; survival stories also turn on ration rules, access control, and failed repairs.

scene_card_examples.visual: At the locked shelter threshold, the lead checks a source-grounded resource meter or access lock and realizes the safe zone is no longer safe.
scene_card_examples.voiceover: The problem is not outside the wall anymore; it is the rule inside it that just failed.

asset_goal.purpose: resource display, threshold, lead posture, survival threat

scene_card_examples.visual: A supply run beat keeps the route, the carried resource, and the blocked return path in one source-grounded frame.
scene_card_examples.voiceover: They did not lose the cargo first; they lost the way back.

asset_goal.purpose: terrain risk, carried resource, return-path problem

script_scene_examples.visual: The lead cancels a direct fight because the source-grounded resource cost would be worse than retreat.
script_scene_examples.voiceover: In the wasteland, surviving the hour can matter more than winning the moment.

dialogue.speaker: lead survivor
dialogue.line: If we spend it here, we do not make it back.

script_scene_examples.action_result: The scene shifts from confrontation to route or resource decision.

dialogue_style_examples: Survival dialogue should name concrete shortages, distances, access, weather, fuel, time, or shelter rules.
Avoid generic hero threats when the real pressure is logistics or exposure.

negative_examples.bad: A random elite convoy or mutant creature appears to make the scene exciting.
negative_examples.why_bad: Unsupported threat escalation breaks source control and continuity planning.

forbidden_drift: Do not replace the lead survivor, companion identity, or worker/suspect role assignment without source support.
Do not add new factions, mutations, weapons, vehicles, or bunker systems unless source-grounded.
Do not turn a resource problem into a combat problem without causal setup.
Do not restore a safe route or safe room in the same beat that proved it was compromised unless source does.
Do not reset alliance status, public/private knowledge state, or route control without an explicit causal change.

image: Lock shelter materials, route terrain, carried resources, and damaged infrastructure as continuity anchors.
video: Use doors, meters, masks, carts, rations, and horizon lines as bridge inserts only when source-grounded.
